turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been coughing nonstop for the past week, and it just won't go away. I've taken dextromethorphan to try to stop the cough, but it hasn't helped much – it still comes back with yellowish-green sputum.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The constant coughing is what's really getting to me. It's a persistent productive cough, started out dry but now I'm bringing up this thick, yellowish-green stuff every time I try to cough or even just talk sometimes.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 5 days ago, and at first, it was just a dry, annoying cough. But over the past few days, it's gotten worse and now I'm coughing up this gross stuff every day.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens all throughout the day, not just at night like I thought it would be. Every time I try to talk, even when I'm just sitting there doing nothing, it comes back up – sometimes a lot, sometimes just a little bit, but it's always something.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 out of 10 - it's really been disrupting my daily life and I'm getting worried about how long this is going to last. The constant coughing and bringing up mucus is just exhausting me.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: When I'm coughing, especially after I bring up that yellowish-green stuff, my chest feels really tight and it's hard to catch my breath. It's like I'm wheezing or something.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It actually gets worse when I'm around a lot of people, like in the classroom where I work with young children every day - I'm worried about exposing them to this, and it's also harder for me to breathe when we're all crowded together. And just walking up stairs or doing anything physical seems to make it worse too.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: Actually, yes - I've been experiencing night sweats every night for the past 4 nights. I'll be lying in bed, and suddenly I'm just soaked through my pajamas, which is really worrying me because I don't know what's causing it or how to stop it.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: The chest tightness and difficulty breathing seem to be mainly centered on my right side, especially when I'm coughing. It feels like a sharp pain that gets worse with deep breathing and coughing.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: I don't think so, I haven't really noticed anything like that. To be honest, I'm just trying to focus on getting rid of this cough and figuring out what's causing it before it gets worse. The night sweats and chest pain are really worrying me right now.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been taking cetirizine 10mg, an over-the-counter allergy medication, as needed for my seasonal allergies. Recently, I've also been taking dextromethorphan to try and suppress the cough, but like I mentioned earlier, it hasn't really helped much.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: I don't think so. To be honest, my last physical exam was about a year ago and everything came back normal. I'm not aware of having any underlying medical conditions. But to be sure, wouldn't it be best to check that out while we're figuring out what's causing this cough?

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I don't think so. To be honest, I haven't really thought about my cholesterol levels before. My last physical exam was a year ago and everything came back normal, but I'm not sure if they checked for that specifically. Can we focus on figuring out what's causing this cough first?

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke. And as for drinking alcohol, I have a glass of wine maybe 1-2 times a week on the weekends, but that's about it.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: Honestly, with this cough, my appetite has been off, so I've been eating pretty light. But before this started, I was actually trying to eat healthier and cook more at home. As for exercise, I try to walk my dog a few times a week, but lately, even that's been tough because of the coughing.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma, actually. That's about it, I don't think we have any history of heart problems on my side of the family.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma, and that's the only condition I can think of. She's actually been dealing with it for a while now, but she seems to manage it okay with her medication.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been about a year since my last physical exam, everything came back normal. To be honest, I'm worried that if this cough isn't serious, maybe I just need to get checked out again and make sure it's not something more going on with my lungs or chest?

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: Yes, I do. I'm allergic to penicillin - it causes a rash every time I take it.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I've been thinking about this a lot lately, and with all these other symptoms like night sweats and chest pain, I'm worried that maybe my cough is more serious than just a simple cold or allergy. My mom's asthma has always made me a bit concerned about respiratory issues in our family, so I'm hoping you can help figure out what's going on here and get to the bottom of it.
